title: Loop Engineering
summary: Designing the agent's outer loop — when to plan, act, verify, and stop — instead of just writing one prompt. Treat the control flow (retries, checkpoints, self-verification, budget-aware stopping) as the real engineering surface.
why: As models get more capable, the leverage moves from the single prompt to the loop around it. A good loop turns a fallible single shot into a reliable system.

title: Context Engineering & the Dumb Zone
summary: Manage the context window as a budget. Quality degrades as utilization climbs — past ~40% (the 'dumb zone') models start losing the plot. Compact, summarize, and offload to keep the working set sharp.
why: Context is the scarcest resource in long agent runs. Foreagent's meters exist precisely to make this visible.

title: Subagent Orchestration
summary: Fan work out to fresh subagents with clean context, each returning a small structured result. Parallelize independent work; isolate risky work; verify with adversarial subagents before committing.
why: One context can't hold everything. Decomposition + independent verification beats a single overloaded agent.

title: Dynamic Workflows
summary: Deterministic control flow (loops, conditionals, fan-out) that decides at runtime how many agents to spawn and what to verify — scaling depth to the task and the budget rather than a fixed script.
why: Static pipelines under-serve hard tasks and over-serve easy ones. Dynamic flows adapt.

title: Agent Skills
summary: Composable, model-invoked capabilities (a folder with a SKILL.md + assets) that load on demand. Skills package domain expertise — APIs, conventions, runbooks — so the agent pulls in exactly what a task needs.
why: Skills turn one-off prompt engineering into reusable, shareable units. This is what the Skills hub here manages.

title: Review Gates & Worktree Isolation
summary: Let agents run autonomously inside throwaway git worktrees, then surface a diff to a human review gate. A bad run is a discarded branch, never a mutated working tree.
why: Isolation is what makes hands-off autonomy safe. It's the core of Foreagent's control plane.

title: Karpathy: software in the agent era
summary: Andrej Karpathy's framing of coding agents as a new computing layer — keep humans in the loop with tight verification, build for partial autonomy, and treat English as a (leaky) programming surface over real tools.
why: A grounded counter to both hype and dismissal: agents are powerful and fallible; design for both.
